title: Receipt
description: The room-authority receipt: one JSONL line per placed run that carried at least one custody: ref. Joins the grant chain (parent -> child, attenuation visible in-receipt), the delivery record, the evidence artifact digests, the custody log selector, and teardown status — self-contained, so a cold reader needs no external store. Vendor-neutral by law: no provider identifiers, no tool names beyond the portfolio's own.

run_id.description: The Runway run this receipt covers.

grants.description: One entry per resolved custody: ref. A run's independent refs are entries here, never separate lines (§8: one derive per (run, ref)).

secret_name.description: The WorkSpec Secret.name this grant resolved.

key.description: The custody key the ref named.

parent_digest.description: Digest of the parent grant record at derive time.

parent_actions.description: The parent's granted actions, so attenuation is visible without an external lookup.

child_id.description: The derived child grant id — the unambiguous selector over custody's log (Evidence.custody_log).

child_digest.description: Digest of the child grant record.

actions.description: The child's granted actions — a subset-or-equal of parent_actions by construction (TDD §4 D1).

bound_source.description: The transport source (room tap address) this grant is usable from (TDD §4 D2b).

channel.description: How the child token reached the guest, e.g. vsock.

one_shot.description: Whether the delivery channel is first-read-then-delete.

artifacts.description: Digest refs into Result.Artifacts (contracts/execution). Type is an OPEN vocabulary — additive within the major version — so rooms can evolve artifact naming without a schema revision.

custody_log.description: One entry per grant: child_id is the unambiguous selector over custody's global, interleaved JSONL log; request_count and lines_sha256 pin what the selector returned at assembly time, so later log tampering is detectable against the receipt.

status.description: unknown is a red flag a reader should surface, not silence (TDD §7 Flow F).
